$comment: Inventory of every redistributed third-party artifact that does NOT arrive as a normal npm registry dependency: vendored data assets under public/, install-time-copied blobs, forked git dependencies, patch-package patches, committed fixtures, and runtime-fetched payloads. npm registry production deps are covered by THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.md (npm run licenses:generate). Schema per entry: name, version, source, license, provenance are REQUIRED (scripts/license-gate.mjs fails CI if any is missing/empty); path, modifications, distribution, notes are optional. Where provenance is genuinely unknown, it is recorded as 'UNKNOWN — needs investigation' rather than guessed. Source analysis: plan/overhaul/analysis/gap-third-party-licensing-provenan.md.

licenseFloor.reason: The combined work MUST be GPL-3.0-or-later: @jofr/capacitor-media-session (npm, GPL-3.0-or-later) and the espeak-ng code/data embedded in the shipped piper_phonemize blobs (GPL-3.0) make any permissive relicensing a license violation, not a cleanup. ua-parser-js v2 (AGPL-3.0-or-later) additionally carries network-source obligations. Do not 'simplify' the package license.

artifacts.name: espeak-ng (code + voice data embedded in piper_phonemize.wasm/.data)
artifacts.version: UNKNOWN — needs investigation (exact espeak-ng commit not recorded by upstream piper-wasm build)
artifacts.provenance: Compiled into piper_phonemize.wasm and packed into piper_phonemize.data (Emscripten FS image of espeak-ng-data) by the upstream DavidCks/piper-wasm build, which states it followed the wide-video/piper-wasm recipe; the exact espeak-ng and piper-phonemize commits are recorded nowhere. GPLv3 §6 corresponding source is therefore currently unidentifiable — long-term fix is rebuilding the WASM from pinned commits in CI (gap report D3).
artifacts.path: public/piper/piper_phonemize.wasm, public/piper/piper_phonemize.data (gitignored; copied from node_modules/piper-wasm at postinstall and shipped in dist/piper/)
artifacts.notes: piper-wasm's blanket 'MIT' package.json claim does not cover these blobs — the binary links GPL-3 espeak-ng, so the artifact is GPL-governed.

artifacts.name: piper-phonemize (compiled into piper_phonemize.wasm)
artifacts.version: UNKNOWN — needs investigation (exact commit not recorded by upstream piper-wasm build)
artifacts.provenance: Compiled to WASM by the upstream DavidCks/piper-wasm build; exact source commit unrecorded (same build chain as the espeak-ng entry).

artifacts.name: piper-wasm build artifacts (piper_phonemize.js loader + piper_worker.js)
artifacts.license: MIT (wrapper/loader code; embedded espeak-ng payload is GPL-3.0 — see espeak-ng entry)
artifacts.provenance: Copied verbatim from node_modules/piper-wasm/build/ into public/piper/ by the prepare-piper postinstall script (package.json); piper_worker.js is then string-patched in place.
artifacts.modifications: scripts/patch_piper_worker.js applies 6 string-replacement patches to piper_worker.js at install time (config injection, phoneme-ID clamping, error handling).

artifacts.name: PT Sans Narrow Web (modified: pinyin tone glyphs injected)
artifacts.provenance: ParaType PT Sans Narrow Web 2.003W; pinyin tone glyphs (ǎ ǐ ǒ ǔ ǚ) were programmatically injected into the TTF binaries using Python fonttools (per README.md Licenses & Attributions). The fonttools script that produced the modified binaries is NOT in the repo — the derivative is currently unreproducible (UNKNOWN — needs investigation).
artifacts.modifications: Glyph injection as above. KNOWN VIOLATION (gap report D2): these are Modified Versions under OFL-1.1 but retain the Reserved Font Names ('PT Sans' family, copyright reserves 'PT Sans'/'PT Serif'/'ParaType') in name table, filenames, and the src/index.css font-family. The rename off the RFNs (with persisted-preference migration) is scheduled for Phase 8 of the overhaul plan; this entry is inventory-only until then.
artifacts.notes: Mitigating: the full OFL-1.1 text and ParaType copyright ride inside the TTF name table, so license text accompanies copies.

artifacts.name: CC-CEDICT (compiled Chinese-English dictionary)
artifacts.version: UNKNOWN — needs investigation (release date/entry-count header is stripped by scripts/compile-dict.cjs, so the shipped release is unknowable)
artifacts.provenance: Compiled from the mdbg.net CC-CEDICT export by scripts/compile-dict.cjs (source URL recorded in the script); the compiler discards the '#' header lines containing the license declaration and release metadata.
artifacts.path: public/dict/cedict.json (14.4 MB, git-tracked; ships in dist/dict/)
artifacts.modifications: Format conversion to JSON lookup table; license/provenance header dropped (gap report D5 — fix is a __meta stamp or sidecar and removing the silent mock-dictionary fallback).
artifacts.notes: Attribution + ShareAlike statement present in README.md and two in-app credits surfaces.

artifacts.name: y-cinder (fork of PodRaven/y-fire)
artifacts.provenance: Personal fork consumed as a pinned git dependency (package.json); retains the upstream MIT LICENSE ('Pod Raven 2024') and declares fork provenance in its README.
artifacts.notes: Slated for in-repo workspace vendoring in Phase 4; the vendoring PR must carry the LICENSE file and a fork-of header per the P0 licensing checklist.

artifacts.name: y-idb (fork of yjs/y-indexeddb)
artifacts.provenance: Personal fork consumed as a pinned git dependency (package.json); retains the upstream MIT LICENSE (Kevin Jahns / RWTH Aachen) and declares fork provenance in its README.
artifacts.notes: Same Phase 4 vendoring checklist as y-cinder.

artifacts.name: zustand-middleware-yjs (fork of joebobmiles/zustand-middleware-yjs)
artifacts.provenance: Personal fork VENDORED into the repo as an npm workspace (Phase 2, plan/overhaul/prep/phase2-fork-surgery.md §6): TS source imported from the fork repo at the exact SHA the app was previously git-pinned to; the fork's committed dist/ at that SHA was verified byte-identical to the previously installed artifact. Retains the upstream MIT LICENSE (Joseph R Miles) verbatim; full lineage, pre-existing fork deltas, and a running Phase 2 modification log live in packages/zustand-middleware-yjs/PROVENANCE.md.
artifacts.path: packages/zustand-middleware-yjs (workspace package; src/ bundled into dist/ at build)
artifacts.modifications: Packaging only at vendor time (peerDependencies for yjs/zustand, exports point at TS source, build/release tooling deleted, jest specs ported to vitest) — zero behavior change, pinned by the contract suite in packages/zustand-middleware-yjs/test/contract/. Subsequent fork surgery is logged in PROVENANCE.md.
artifacts.notes: package.json stays private:true (never published), which keeps it OUT of license-checker output — this inventory entry plus the in-tree LICENSE file are its license record. y-idb and y-cinder remain git-pinned until their own phases.

artifacts.name: @capgo/capacitor-social-login local patch (login_hint passthrough)
artifacts.provenance: Hand-written patch-package diff modifying dist/esm/google-provider.js of the MPL-2.0 upstream; applied by postinstall. The patch file in this public repo is the MPL §3.2 source-availability mechanism for the modification. No upstream PR reference recorded (gap report D6 — should be upstreamed).
artifacts.modifications: Adds login_hint plumbing to the Google OAuth provider (ESM dist only; CJS and .d.ts untouched).

artifacts.name: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland (demo EPUB, Project Gutenberg #11)
artifacts.license: Public domain (Project Gutenberg License governs use of the embedded PG trademark text)
artifacts.provenance: Downloaded from Project Gutenberg; exact download date/edition unrecorded.
artifacts.path: public/alice.epub, public/books/alice.epub (byte-identical duplicates, both ship in dist/), src/test/fixtures/alice.epub, verification/alice.epub

artifacts.name: Project Gutenberg test-fixture EPUBs (frankenstein, jane-eyre, pride-and-prejudice, room-with-a-view)
artifacts.version: UNKNOWN — needs investigation (PG eBook numbers/editions unrecorded)
artifacts.license: Public domain (Project Gutenberg License governs use of the embedded PG trademark text)
artifacts.provenance: Committed as Playwright E2E fixtures; exact Project Gutenberg editions and download dates unrecorded. Not distributed in any build output (verification/ is test-only).

artifacts.name: Google Drive product logo
artifacts.license: Proprietary — Google trademark/brand asset (not open-source licensed; used nominatively to identify the Google Drive integration)
artifacts.provenance: Google brand asset; exact origin and brand-guideline review unrecorded (gap report D9).

artifacts.name: Background-audio media assets (silence.ogg, 10s_8k_sub_bass_vbr_off.webm)
artifacts.source: UNKNOWN — needs investigation (src/assets/README.md does not document them)
artifacts.license: UNKNOWN — needs investigation (likely first-party-generated trivial audio, but unrecorded)
artifacts.provenance: UNKNOWN — needs investigation. Fix per gap report D9: record origin or regenerate with a documented ffmpeg command.

artifacts.name: Bible book-name lexicon data (bible-lexicon.ts)
artifacts.source: UNKNOWN — needs investigation (src/data/bible-lexicon.ts:73 cites a 'lexicon-bible.csv' that does not exist in the repo)
artifacts.license: UNKNOWN — needs investigation (content is factual abbreviation data, so copyright risk is minimal, but the citation is dead)
artifacts.provenance: UNKNOWN — needs investigation. Fix per gap report D9: commit the CSV or rewrite the comment as hand-assembled.
artifacts.path: src/data/bible-lexicon.ts (2,899 lines of data; bundled into dist/)

artifacts.name: Piper voice models (runtime-fetched, per-user)
artifacts.license: Heterogeneous per-voice dataset licenses (several upstream MODEL_CARDs are non-commercial or attribution-bearing); NOT redistributed by Versicle — fetched directly by the user's browser on demand
artifacts.provenance: Fetched at runtime from https://huggingface.co/rhasspy/piper-voices/resolve/v1.0.0/ (src/lib/tts/providers/PiperProvider.ts). User-initiated download, not Versicle redistribution; the voice picker surfaces no per-voice license info (gap report D10 product gap).
artifacts.path: (runtime fetch only; cached in user's browser storage)

artifacts.name: onnxruntime-web (runtime-fetched from CDN)
artifacts.provenance: Loaded at runtime from cdnjs (src/lib/tts/providers/piper-utils.ts); not bundled or redistributed today. If/when vendored into public/piper/ (tts-providers target design), it must gain a redistributed-artifact entry here and in THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.
